summary: Problem 6.4 asks to improve the O(k^2) upper bound on f_h(H) for a Hamilton cycle H in complete or complete bipartite graphs. The source paper establishes this bound via a necklace-splitting argument, noting that any improvement to the bipartite matching bound yields an equal improvement for complete graphs. The paper was posted in April 2026, and no follow-up work addressing this problem has been found in the literature.
search_queries: colour-balanced subgraphs Hamilton cycle f_h bounds complete bipartite graph 2604.09449
Hogan Scott Tsarev colour-balanced subgraphs necklace-splitting Hamilton cycle 2026
notes: No follow-up found. The paper was posted 2026-04-10 and as of 2026-05-14 is only ~5 weeks old; absence of follow-up is expected. The conjecture is tightly coupled to the bipartite matching bound via necklace-splitting, so progress on either sub-problem would resolve both.
